evaluations.prompt: Inside the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event, rank the 8 most-active national outcomes by combined live tradability — show YES price, bid-ask spread, $5k exit slippage, 24h volume, and total whale dollars per nation so I can see which World Cup contracts combine tight quotes with real conviction and which are headline-only.
hardFailures: no chart artifact was emitted
semantic chart audit failed: semantic audit expected a chart but no chart summary existed
evaluations.improvementLever: Replace with a prompt whose answer naturally renders as a horizontal bar chart of $5k slippage by nation, or downgrade the requested metric set so the executor isn't forced to choose between table and chart. The data is genuinely strong — the failure is purely visual.

evaluations.prompt: Across the 'What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in May 2026?' strike ladder, compare the YES probability and 24h trading volume for the $90, $100, $110, $120, $130, $140, and $150 HIGH strikes — chart the implied probability curve so I can see exactly where the market expects WTI to settle this month and where the volume actually is.
hardFailures: semantic chart audit failed: no chart matched the prompt's requested axis, chart type, and metrics
semantic chart audit failed: chart 0: probability prompt did not chart a probability, odds, yes-price, or price series
evaluations.improvementLever: Replace with a strike-ladder prompt that asks ONLY for the implied probability curve (single metric) so the executor can't conflate volume and probability. The current ↑/↓ mixed-direction strikes also confuse the categorical axis. A cleaner 'will WTI close above ___' single-side ladder would chart cleanly.

evaluations.prompt: For the highest-volume Polymarket binary right now — 'Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?' (~$5.8M 24h volume, YES at $0.014) — break down the last 48 hours of trading into Small (<$50), Medium ($50-$500), and Whale (>$1k) buckets. Chart buy volume, sell volume, net flow, and trade count per bucket so I can see whether whales and retail are agreeing or diverging on this BTC longshot.
hardFailures: no chart artifact was emitted
response text signals sparse/no-value output
quotedEvidence: An inline chart was not rendered for this turn
evaluations.improvementLever: The 3-bucket structure may be too few rows to feel chart-worthy to the executor, even though 3 grouped bars (buy/sell/net per bucket) would chart fine. Replace with a top-N-trader concentration prompt or a tape-by-hour breakdown so the executor naturally produces 8+ rows.

Replace with a sport whose team list is fully contained in one Polymarket event (e.g. NHL playoff bracket), or pre-resolve the candidate list against the live event before sending to the executor.

evaluations.prompt: Show me the top 10 trending crypto markets on Polymarket right now — for each, give the title, current YES price, 24h volume, trend score, and a short explanation of why activity is unusual today. I want to see which crypto narratives are catching fire vs which are bleeding.

Bitcoin Up or Down on May 5? | 0.795 | $112,043.88 | 95 | Very large 30x above-average volume suggests a fresh catalyst
evaluations.improvementLever: The response is genuinely strong but rendered as a markdown table with no chart. Replace with 'top 10 by 24h volume change %' framed as a horizontal bar chart of trend scores — this nudges the executor toward chart output rather than table-only.

evaluations.prompt: Inside the 'Eurovision Winner 2026' event with the contest only ~10 days out, list the 12 most-traded national entries with current YES probability and 24h volume per outcome so I can see the implied podium distribution and which nations the market is actually concentrating on.

Reframe the prompt to explicitly say 'horizontal bar chart by country', or replace with a Eurovision-narrowed prompt (e.g. top 5 favorites only) to force outcome-name x-axis. Genuine buyer insight (volume on longshots, not favorites) is wasted by broken chart.

evaluations.prompt: Pull the maximum-history price chart at daily fidelity for the YES token on 'Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?' Plot the implied probability over time so I can see how the curve has compressed or run as the deadline has approached and BTC spot has moved.
quotedEvidence: the implied probability for Bitcoin hitting $150,000 by June 30, 2026, has experienced a near-total compression, falling from a peak of 67% in August 2025 to just 1.35% today
evaluations.improvementLever: Showcase-ready as-is. Use this as the gold-standard template: single-token max-history time series with date x-axis and implied-probability y-axis, plus a market-summary table with all-time high/low/net change.

evaluations.prompt: For the 'Will England win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?' contract (currently ~11% YES), compare against its sibling national outcomes (Spain, France, South Korea, Switzerland, New Zealand, Haiti) inside the same World Cup event — show price, spread, and 24h volume per sibling so I can see whether England's quote is rich, cheap, or fairly priced vs the rest of the field.

Replace with a prompt asking for a stacked horizontal bar of YES vs NO total supply by top-10 holder rank — that forces a chart and preserves the conviction story.
